original_hypothesis.title: DelayBuffer.isUpdatable uses block.number - prevBlockNumber subtraction without guarding prevBlockNumber initialization, risking revert/incorrect sync logic after misconfigured init
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: DelayBuffer.isSynced() computes block.number - self.prevBlockNumber <= self.threshold. If prevBlockNumber is uninitialized (0) or set improperly, the sync window logic may behave unexpectedly (always synced until block.number grows large) or could revert if future refactors change types. While current uint64 subtraction is safe due to Solidity 0.8 checked arithmetic? (it will revert on underflow only if prevBlockNumber > block.number, which could occur if updated with a future blockNumber). Mis-set prevBlockNumber (e.g., via _setBufferConfig update using block.number cast) could break delay-proof requirements and liveness.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Buffer sync state gates whether delay proofs are required. Any bug in initialization or updates to prevBlockNumber/prevSequencedBlockNumber can cause incorrect enforcement, potentially allowing delayed-message reads without proofs or forcing proofs unnecessarily (DoS).
deployment_assumptions: BufferData.prevBlockNumber or prevSequencedBlockNumber can be set to an unexpected value via upgrade/init path or malformed delayProof.delayedMessage.blockNumber accepted by Messages.isValidDelayedAccPreimage.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: SequencerInbox._setBufferConfig conditionally calls buffer.update(uint64(block.number)); DelayBuffer.update sets prevBlockNumber to provided blockNumber (which may come from delayed message proof).

proof_of_signal.pattern: isSynced: return block.number - self.prevBlockNumber <= self.threshold; prevBlockNumber is mutable and set from external-sourced blockNumber in update()

preconditions: isDelayBufferable enabled and buffer is used to gate delay proofs
prevBlockNumber becomes > block.number (future block) OR otherwise inconsistent with actual delayed message sequencing

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: sync gating relies on arithmetic with mutable reference points
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: incorrect buffer sync state can bypass or over-enforce DelayProofRequired gating
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no explicit check that update(blockNumber) <= block.number; relies on upstream proof validity

analysis_questions: Can delayProof.delayedMessage.blockNumber ever be greater than current block.number while still passing Messages.isValidDelayedAccPreimage?
Are there upgrade/initialization paths where prevBlockNumber remains 0 or is set incorrectly leading to long-lived 'synced' state?
Does SequencerInbox.forceInclusion use l1BlockAndTime[0] that could be in the future relative to current block.number?
validation_requirements: Unit-test DelayBuffer.update with blockNumber > current block.number and observe isSynced behavior/reverts
Audit Messages.Message.blockNumber semantics and how Bridge populates it

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If an attacker can cause prevBlockNumber to be set ahead of block.number, then isSynced subtraction underflows/reverts, potentially bricking batch posting paths that consult buffer.isSynced/isUpdatable (DoS).
prerequisites: Ability to get a future blockNumber accepted in buffer.update via delay proof or force inclusion parameterization

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Indirect: could halt batch posting or force inclusion under certain states
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All rollup users during the affected period
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: May require admin upgrade or buffer config reset

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing adversarial tests for future/invalid blockNumber inputs to buffer.update

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
